release_review.summary: Gateway is now a separate redeven-gateway service and protocol; Runtime Service protocol compatibility stays on the existing window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1 and runtime-control/open flows continue to use the existing desktop-bridge contract for env runtimes
Gateway catalog, profile management, pairing, and open-session now use the separate redeven-gateway-v1 protocol and redeven-gateway binary rather than Runtime Service snapshot fields
Desktop Gateway management actions use the independent Gateway service package slot and state root; env runtime start/stop/update actions remain separate
Older redeven-runtime-gateway-v1 catalog responses are rejected by Gateway protocol validation instead of being consumed as normal environments
Gateway catalog no longer exposes env_local by default; Gateway host environments must be explicit Gateway-owned profiles
Compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, and minimum Runtime version remain unchanged because Runtime Service's own wire contract is not widened in this change
release_review.same_window_rationale: Gateway service management is outside the Runtime Service protocol window: it uses redeven-gateway-v1, a separate binary, and a separate managed package slot. Env runtime operations still use the existing Runtime Service protocol and desktop bridge, so no Runtime Service compatibility epoch or minimum version change is required for this release contract.
